name: Uqba ibn Sam'an
title: Servant of Rubab
shortDescription: The servant of Lady Rubab (SA) who survived Karbala and narrated many details of the journey and the events of Ashura to history.
fullStory:
Uqba ibn Sam'an was the servant of Lady Rubab (Imam Hussain's wife and Ali Asghar's mother). He accompanied the caravan from the very beginning of the departure from Medina to Karbala, and because he was attached to the Imam's family, he witnessed many of the Imam's words, letters, and hidden decisions firsthand.

On the day of Ashura, he was also alongside the others, but because he did not wield a weapon or was recognized as a servant, he was not killed. After the Imam's martyrdom, Umar Sa'd's army arrested him.

Umar Sa'd asked him: 'Who are you?' Uqba replied: 'I am a mamluk (servant).' According to the customs of the time, they spared the lives of servants; therefore, Umar Sa'd released him.

Uqba ibn Sam'an later became known as one of the most important first-hand narrators of the history of Karbala. It was he who categorically refuted false rumors (like Imam Hussain proposing to surrender to Yazid) and delivered the truth to the ears of history.
quoteArabic: وَاللَّهِ ما أَعْطاهُمُ الحُسَيْنُ أَنْ يَضَعَ يَدَهُ فِي يَدِ يَزِيدَ...
quote: (Uqba's historical testimony:) By God, Hussain never proposed to put his hand in Yazid's hand, rather he said: 'Leave me to go in this vast earth.'
